claim: Adaptive tau (set below mixing time) prevents rank-1 macro kernel collapse
falsification_criterion: Macro kernels remain rank-1 even with tau < mixing time
result: Pre-rank-1 regime exists at tau=1 only (frob 0.07-0.19). By tau=2 frob drops 10-30x. Window is 1 step wide — too narrow for adaptive tau alone. Root gap 0.885-0.962 means mixing in ~1 step.

claim: Coarse-grained macro process has significant non-Markovianity (memory)
falsification_criterion: P(X_{t+1}|X_t) matches P(X_{t+1}|X_t,X_{t-1}) within noise
result: Non-Markovianity is real but conditional on macro_n. Low macro_n (3-6): nm_mean<0.03. High macro_n (13-51, 28% of runs): nm_mean 0.1-0.3. r=0.99 correlation with macro_n. Increases with scale.

claim: Slow-mode-preserving merges produce non-trivial L2+ macro kernels while maintaining DPI
falsification_criterion: Either all merges are rank-1 or DPI is violated
result: dev=0 for ALL 80 runs. Ctrl and slow identical in 39/40 runs. Merge criterion does not matter — rank-1 is structural, not a consequence of merge ordering.

claim: Self-modifying dynamics (P6 budget drive + mixture kernel) produce non-rank-1 macro kernels via spectral bisection lens
falsification_criterion: Frobenius distance from rank-1 stays at null baseline across all scales
result: max_frob 3-5x above null at all scales. n=32: 0.323±0.026, n=64: 0.161±0.010, n=128: 0.094±0.005. But max_frob halves per doubling of n — effect is scale-dependent and decays.

claim: P3 protocol phase biasing amplifies P6-driven structure creation
falsification_criterion: EXP-079 (P6+P3) max_frob no higher than EXP-077 (P6 alone)
result: P6+P3 > P6-alone: +26% max_frob at n=32 (0.408 vs 0.323), +8% at n=64, +6% at n=128. Effect diminishes with scale. P3 alone (EXP-078) = null baseline.

claim: P6 budget is the essential activation mechanism for kernel self-modification
falsification_criterion: P3-only (EXP-078) produces modifications without P6 budget
result: P3-only (EXP-078) produces ZERO modifications in all 30 runs — identical to null baseline (EXP-076). P6 budget is necessary and sufficient for modifications.

claim: P6-driven structure creation scales (maintains or increases) with system size
falsification_criterion: max_frob decreases with n
result: max_frob halves per doubling: 0.408 (n=32) → 0.167 (n=64) → 0.099 (n=128) → 0.063 (n=256 spot check). Random P2 gating insufficient at large n — each flip affects O(1/n^2) of edges.

claim: Spectral-guided P2 gating (targeting inter-cluster edges via 2nd eigenvector) produces scale-independent non-rank-1 macro structure
falsification_criterion: max_frob still decreases with n, or stays at null baseline
result: max_frob FLAT across all scales: n=32: 0.861±0.002, n=64: 0.859±0.000, n=128: 0.858±0.000. eff_gap drops to O(10^-4). Adaptive tau rises to O(10^3). Universal attractor at frob≈0.858. ALL 30 runs converge. Scaling problem SOLVED.

claim: Budget cap (at initial value n*ln(n)) does not affect the spectral-guided attractor
falsification_criterion: max_frob with budget cap significantly lower than EXP-080 (uncapped)
result: max_frob identical to uncapped: n=32: 0.862±0.002, n=64: 0.859±0.000, n=128: 0.858±0.000. Budget stays at cap. Unbounded budget not needed for attractor convergence.

claim: Multi-state spectral partition (k=4, sign-based on v2,v3) produces richer macro structure than bisection
falsification_criterion: max_frob with k=4 not higher than k=2
result: max_frob 1.4-1.5x higher than k=2: n=32: 1.437 (vs 0.861), n=64: 1.277 (vs 0.859), n=128: 1.456 (vs 0.858). macro_n=3 (one of 4 clusters typically empty). Scale-independent. Richer structure confirmed.

claim: Evolved kernel (after spectral-guided dynamics) produces near-Markov macro process under bisection lens
falsification_criterion: nm_mean > 0.01 for the evolved kernel's macro process
result: nm_mean < 10^-4 at all scales. Peak nm_max < 10^-3. Spectral-guided gating creates near-lumpable partition — the macro process IS Markov. Contrasts with EXP-074 (original kernel with high-macro_n partition has nm 0.1-0.3).

claim: k=8 spectral partition (3 eigenvectors) produces even richer macro structure than k=4
falsification_criterion: max_frob with k=8 not higher than k=4
result: max_frob: n=32: 1.969 (vs 1.437 for k=4), n=64: 1.788, n=128: 1.816. macro_n=4-5 (not 8 — empty clusters). Scale-independent. Frob increases with k but saturates.

claim: k=4 with budget cap is a viable recommended configuration
falsification_criterion: Budget cap significantly reduces max_frob compared to uncapped k=4
result: max_frob: n=32: 1.467±0.225, n=64: 1.392±0.208, n=128: 1.459±0.141. Comparable to uncapped EXP-082. Budget cap viable for k=4. macro_n=3 (same as uncapped).

claim: Evolved kernel with k=4 spectral partition also produces near-Markov macro process
falsification_criterion: nm_mean > 0.01 for the evolved k=4 macro process
result: nm_mean < 10^-4 at all scales (32: 4e-5, 64: 2e-5, 128: 7e-5). Peak nm_max < 10^-3. Even with 3 macro states, the spectral-guided gating creates near-lumpable partitions.

claim: Cross-layer coupling (Level 1 P6 audit modulating Level 0 P2 cost) amplifies macro structure above the uncoupled baseline
falsification_criterion: max_frob with coupling not higher than EXP-085 (uncoupled) at any scale
result: Coupling constrains P2 accept rate (41-86% vs 99%+) but does NOT amplify frob. n=32: 1.438±0.158 (vs 1.467), n=64: 1.445±0.168 (vs 1.392), n=128: 1.319±0.208 (vs 1.459). Comparable at small n, slightly lower at n=128. Coupling acts as stabilizer, not amplifier.

claim: Evolved macro kernel (3-state, from Level 0 spectral-guided dynamics) produces non-rank-1 Level 2 macro kernel when further coarse-grained via spectral bisection + adaptive tau
falsification_criterion: L1 frob < 0.1 for all seeds and scales (rank-1 at Level 2)
result: L1 frob substantial at all scales: n=32: 0.972±0.228, n=64: 0.722±0.335, n=128: 0.880±0.197. Min across all 30 runs: 0.264. ALL non-zero. Two-level emergence ladder confirmed.

claim: Level 1 dynamics on 3-state macro kernel are trivially constrained by viability checks (no modifications accepted)
falsification_criterion: P1 or P2 modifications accepted at Level 1
result: Zero P1 and P2 modifications in quick test. Macro kernel has near-absorbing state (K[0][0]=1.0) violating max_self_loop. Viability constraints designed for n>>3 prevent any evolution. Level 1 dynamics reduce to static cascade.

claim: Three-level emergence ladder: k=8 dynamics at L0 produce 4-5 state macro kernel, k=4 static partition gives 3-4 state L1 macro, bisection gives 2-state L2 macro — all non-rank-1
falsification_criterion: L2 frob < 0.1 for majority of runs (rank-1 at Level 2)

claim: L2 frob variance is driven by L0 macro kernel block-diagonality (spectral gap), not by L0 frob magnitude
falsification_criterion: L0_macro_gap has no correlation with L2_frob, or L0_frob is the better predictor
result: r(L0_macro_gap, L2_frob) = -0.60. BLOCK (gap<0.001): L2 frob=1.07±0.26 (n=20). NON-BLOCK (gap>0.001): L2 frob=0.51±0.27 (n=10). r(L0_frob, L2_frob) = 0.05 (no predictive power). Near-block-diagonal macro kernels propagate structure through more levels.

claim: Both L0 and L1 macro processes in the three-level ladder are near-Markov (non-Markovianity negligible)
falsification_criterion: L1 nm_mean > 0.01 (significant memory in the macro-of-macro process)
result: L0 nm: max 0.010, mean <0.001. L1 nm: max 0.0001, mean <1e-5. Only 1/30 runs has L1 nm > 1e-4. Both levels are essentially Markov. Spectral-guided gating creates near-lumpable partitions at every level.

claim: Coupling strength modulates P2 acceptance rate but does not change the max_frob attractor
falsification_criterion: max_frob systematically increases or decreases with coupling strength across {0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20}
result: No trend. Mean max_frob across all strengths: n=32: 1.37-1.47, n=64: 1.39-1.45. All within 1 std. Accept rate drops monotonically at n=64: 0.645 (uncoupled) to 0.573 (strength=20). Coupling constrains but does not reshape the attractor.

claim: Partition entropy (MI between micro and macro levels) reveals a bimodal attractor landscape: ~50% of runs have balanced partitions (h_l0 ≈ ln(2)), ~50% have degenerate partitions (h_l0 ≈ 0)
falsification_criterion: h_l0 is unimodal across seeds (no bimodal split in partition entropy)
result: Clean bimodal split at all scales. n=32: 5/10 h_l0>0.1, 5/10 h_l0<0.1. Gap between modes: 0 to 0.6 (nothing in between at n=32). When h_l0>0.1 (balanced): frac_chain≈1.0 (perfect L0→L1 information preservation). Frob is high regardless (~1.2) — frob measures transition structure while entropy measures occupancy balance.

claim: Phase 1 emergent properties (chirality, MI, spectral lines, locality, DPI cascade, route mismatch) are genuinely non-trivial on Phase 2 evolved non-rank-1 macro kernels at all three levels of the emergence ladder
falsification_criterion: Majority of properties remain at noise levels (sigma < 0.001, MI < 0.01, only 1 non-zero eigenvalue) despite non-rank-1 frob
result: 3/6 positive: MI (0.63-0.69 nats at L0 for block-mode), spectral lines (2-3 non-trivial eigenvalues at all seeds), route mismatch (increases L0→L1→L2). 3/6 negative: chirality σ≈0 (reversible macro kernels), 1D locality = 0 (block interleaving), DPI vacuous.

claim: Phase 1 emergent properties (chirality, MI, spectral lines, locality, DPI cascade, route mismatch) are genuinely non-trivial on Phase 2 evolved non-rank-1 macro kernels at all three levels of the emergence ladder
falsification_criterion: Majority of properties remain at noise levels (sigma < 0.001, MI < 0.01, only 1 non-zero eigenvalue) despite non-rank-1 frob
result: CLOSED (CLO-102d): 1.5/6 positive under π-weighting; uniform-weighted diagnostics show rich structure (CLO-102b). Chirality definitively absent — confirmed by 3 independent metrics (cycle affinity, transient EP, Frobenius asymmetry). Macro kernels have funnel topology: reversible transient core + absorbing sinks. Frobenius asymmetry detects one-way edges but these are directed flow, not directed cycles. Spectral lines and route mismatch are the genuine positive findings. MI measures π-entropy (bimodal), not transition predictability.

claim: Signed boundary repair coupling (SBRC) increases sustained level1_frob and/or P2 acceptance rate vs sign-blind coupling, because repairs (structure-reinforcing flips) are no longer penalized
falsification_criterion: SBRC (coupling_signed=true) produces neither higher level1_frob nor higher P2 acceptance rate compared to EXP-087 (sign-blind coupling) at any scale
result: CLO-103: Scale-dependent frob (worse at n=64, better at n=128, net delta +0.023). P2 accept rate uniformly LOWER. HYP-123 NOT SUPPORTED.

claim: SBRC coupling does not change the qualitative ladder structure: three-level non-rank-1 ladder still forms, and property diagnostics (absorbing states, eigenvalues, chirality absence) are unchanged vs uncoupled baseline (EXP-090/094)
falsification_criterion: SBRC ladder fails to produce 3-level non-rank-1 structure, or property diagnostics show qualitatively different behavior

claim: P6→P3 mixer (frob-driven protocol modulation) increases sustained frob by adaptively boosting exploration when macro structure collapses and consolidating when structure is rich
falsification_criterion: Mixer-enabled runs produce no higher frob than SBRC-only (EXP-096) baseline at any strength or scale
result: CLO-105 (revised): Frob NOT amplified, but full diagnostics (EXP-099) show large topology changes: fewer absorbing states (d=-1.08), larger spectral gap (d=+1.19), lower pi-MI (d=-1.24), more chirality (d=+0.83). Preliminary n=32 only.

claim: Individual PICA cells produce distinct diagnostic signatures when enabled alone: each of the 13 action cells changes at least one macro diagnostic with effect size |d|>0.5 compared to baseline (P2<-P4 only)
falsification_criterion: All single-cell runs produce diagnostics indistinguishable from baseline (P2<-P4 only)

claim: Multi-level PICA dynamics (P1-P6 applied at all ladder levels with inter-level coupling) produces qualitatively richer structure than static macro derivation: higher L1/L2 frob, more chirality, or different macro topology
falsification_criterion: Multi-level dynamics output indistinguishable from static derivation (EXP-090 baseline)

claim: Most new PICA cells (A18-A23) are inert when enabled alone on top of baseline because they lack either populated informant data (cluster_rm) or output consumers (A5/A11 for packaging). The PICA is a dependency graph: cells need both inputs and outputs to affect dynamics.
falsification_criterion: A cell in A18-A23 produces dynamics different from baseline when enabled alone
result: CONFIRMED: A18, A19, A20, A21, A22, A23, P5_row_all all produce dynamics identical to baseline (same p1_acc, p2_acc, budget to last digit). Root causes: (1) A19/A20 need cluster_rm but no P3-informant cell populates it; (2) A21-A23 produce packaging but no consumer (A5/A11) is enabled; (3) A18 writes active_tau but tau doesn't feed back into dynamics.

claim: A13 (P3←P6 frob mixer) is the primary driver of P3_row_all's dramatic effect (sigma_pi→0, frob→1.4). Adding A18+A19+A20 provides secondary modulation but A13 alone should produce most of the effect.
falsification_criterion: A13_only produces dynamics identical to baseline, or P3_row_all minus A13 still produces its effect

claim: A25 (P6←P6 DPI budget cap) is inert because DPI is always satisfied at n=64 with baseline dynamics. The cap_mult clamp of [0.3, 1.0] means it can only tighten, never boost, and DPI ratio < 1 returns identity.
falsification_criterion: A25 produces non-identity P6Scores (cap_mult < 1.0) in at least one seed
result: CONFIRMED: P6_row_all = A24_only exactly across all 10 seeds. A25 has zero effect because baseline dynamics always satisfy DPI (B12 dpi_ratio ≈ 0.006, well below 1.0 threshold). The cap_mult = 1.0 for all runs.

claim: Packaging consumer+producer combos (A5+A21, A11+A22) should change dynamics because packaging is produced AND consumed. The consumer reads active_packaging to modulate P1/P2 targeting.
falsification_criterion: A5+A21 and A11+A22 produce dynamics identical to baseline

claim: A13 is the primary driver of P3_row_all. (REVISED: A13 alone provides mild effect; the dramatic sigma_pi→0 phase transition requires A13+A18+A19 chain.)
result: A13_only: frob 0.898 (+0.064 vs baseline), mild effect. A13+A18 = A13_only (A18 adds nothing). A13+A18+A19: frob 1.340 (+0.506), sigma_pi=0. The critical chain is A13 (initial push) + A18 (populates cluster_rm) + A19 (sector-specific mixture amplification).

claim: Packaging consumer+producer combos change dynamics.
result: CONFIRMED: A5+A21 (frob -0.032), A11+A22 (frob +0.088, sigma_pi 8.27), A11+A21 (frob +0.056). All differ from baseline. Effects are small but real. A11+A22 produces the highest irreversibility (sigma_pi=8.27, 2.5x baseline).

claim: The sigma_pi→0 (reversible macro) phase transition requires the A13+A18+A19 chain: A13 shifts mixture weights from frob, A18 populates cluster_rm as a side effect, A19 uses cluster_rm for sector-specific mixture modulation. Without A19, the system stays in the normal regime.
falsification_criterion: sigma_pi→0 occurs without A19, or A13+A18+A19 doesn't produce sigma_pi→0
result: CONFIRMED: A13_only→sigma_pi=5.602, A13+A18→5.602, A13+A18+A19→0.000. A19 is the critical threshold.

claim: The sigma_pi→0 transition is a binary switch: the mere presence of A19 (sector mixing) triggers it regardless of its strength parameter (p3_p4_sector_boost). All boost values 0.1-4.0 produce identical dynamics including p2_rate, budget, and macro kernel.
falsification_criterion: Different boost values produce different sigma_pi values
result: CONFIRMED: sigma_pi=0.000, frob=1.340, p2_rate=0.1281, budget=266.2 for ALL boost values. Not a gradual transition — binary switch.

claim: SBRC (A12, repair/violation mechanism) breaks the reversible regime by introducing directional asymmetry in P2 gating. The reversible chain + SBRC produces sigma_pi=1.68 instead of 0.
falsification_criterion: chain+SBRC still produces sigma_pi=0
result: CONFIRMED: chain_SBRC has sigma=1.682, repair_frac=0.62, budget=195.8 (not saturated). SBRC's repair/violation mechanism introduces directional bias that prevents the system from reaching the reversible fixed point.

claim: A14 (P4←P3, RM-quantile partition) disrupts the reversible chain because it changes the partition used by all downstream computations, including A19 itself. When the partition changes, the sector-specific RM landscape shifts, preventing the stable fixed point.
falsification_criterion: A13+A14+A19 produces sigma_pi=0
result: CONFIRMED: A13+A14+A19 has sigma=3.08 (not reversible). A14's RM-quantile partition competes with A15's spectral partition, creating instability. In contrast, A3/A18/A21 don't change the partition and the chain works normally.

claim: The reversible transition (sigma_pi→0 from A13+A18+A19) persists at larger scales
evidence: EXP-107: At n=64 sigma=0.000, at n=128 sigma=1.380, at n=256 sigma=0.202. The transition is n=64-specific. Baseline sigma itself drops 3.3→2.2→0.2, making the chain's modulation marginal at scale.

claim: A25 becomes active at larger scales because DPI violations occur
evidence: EXP-107: A25_only = baseline at n=64, n=128, AND n=256. DPI is never violated at any tested scale.

claim: full_action is scale-resilient: maintains high frob and low sigma across scales
evidence: EXP-107: full_action frob 1.081→1.134→1.062 (stays above 1.0 while all others drop below 1.0 at n=256). sigma 0.000→0.165→0.297. p2_rate 0.131→0.126→0.177. Repair fraction stable at ~0.52.

claim: Baseline sigma_pi decreases with scale (macro kernel naturally tends toward reversibility at large n)
evidence: EXP-107: Baseline sigma 3.342 (n=64) → 2.218 (n=128) → 0.214 (n=256). All configs show same decreasing trend.

claim: Baseline frob is non-monotonic with scale (increases then decreases)
evidence: EXP-107: Baseline frob 0.834 (n=64) → 0.979 (n=128) → 0.514 (n=256). Peaks at n=128 then drops, suggesting k=8 macro states are too few for n=256.

claim: A18 (P3<-P3 adaptive tau) is needed as cluster_rm enabler for A19 to trigger reversible transition
evidence: EXP-104v3 post-fix: A19_only triggers sigma=0 (9/10 seeds) without A18. Bug #1 fix (needs_cluster_rm includes A19) made A18 unnecessary.

claim: A19 (P3<-P4 sector mixing) alone triggers the reversible transition at n=64
evidence: EXP-104v3: A19_only gives frob=1.34, sigma=0.18 (9/10 seeds sigma=0.00). EXP-105v2: A13+A18+A19 gives sigma=0.42 (8/10 sigma=0). The transition persists regardless of other cells.

claim: A13 (P3<-P6 frob mixer) is the strongest sigma booster at n=64
evidence: EXP-105v2: A13_only mean sigma=8.11 (+143% over baseline 3.34). A11_A22 is comparable (8.27) but A13 is a single cell.

claim: P1_row_A13 is the best all-round configuration (high frob + high sigma)
evidence: EXP-105v2 at n=64: frob=0.999, sigma=6.15. But needs scale test (EXP-107v2).

claim: A19 and A13 are antagonistic: A19 creates structure (high frob) but kills irreversibility; A13 boosts irreversibility but not structure
evidence: EXP-105v2: A13 alone sigma=8.11, frob=0.836. A19 alone sigma=0.18, frob=1.339. Together (chain) sigma=0.42, frob=1.189. A19 dominates the interaction.

claim: PICA cell effects diminish dramatically at larger scales (n=128, n=256)
evidence: EXP-107v2 early results: A13_only sigma drops from 8.11 (n=64) to 1.19 (n=128). Chain sigma goes from 0.42 to 1.16. Effects are converging. Pending full n=128 and n=256 data.

claim: Rich audit records (sigma_ratio, flip counts, multi-scale scan) will reveal cell utility profiles, interaction networks, and emergent phenotypes beyond what frob/sigma alone can measure. Scale-dependent effects require multi-metric characterization.
falsification_criterion: sigma_ratio and flip counts provide no new information beyond frob/sigma for distinguishing cell effects

claim: Rich audit records reveal cell utility profiles, interaction networks, and emergent phenotypes beyond frob/sigma alone. SUPPORTED: sigma_ratio uniquely identifies full_action (0.488 vs <0.02 for all others). Flip counts reveal three partition clusters. Multi-scale scans reveal two qualitative regimes. gated_edges is strongest frob predictor (r=-0.51). REV/NRM bifurcation visible at k=2 (micro-kernel level). 37 findings total.

At n=256, full_action maintains a scale-independent ~2x frob advantage over baseline, but the REV/NRM bifurcation collapses (0% REV). The full 25-cell collective PICA system operates in a qualitatively different regime that resists scale convergence, while all subsets converge to the same baseline attractor. Candidate theorem: for any non-full PICA subset S, lim_{n->inf} frob(S)/frob(baseline) = 1.
evidence: F42-F44 supported full_action advantage and REV collapse. BUT F46 REFUTES the universality claim: A14_only (3/3 >1.0, median 1.157), A16_only (3/3, 1.171), A17_only (2/3, 1.372) all EXCEED full_action (1.075) at n=256. Scale ratio >1.0 (frob INCREASES from n=128). Partition competition with any alternative lens is sufficient — the full 25-cell collective is NOT required. The candidate theorem is FALSE.
tests_remaining: EXP-106 n=256 (10-seed statistics for full_action, A13_A14_A19)

claim: At coarse-graining scales where PICA produces high frob (k=4-8), the PLA2 dominance gap is significantly smaller than at baseline, indicating emergent saddle-point dominated path measures (classical limit)
falsification_criterion: FALSIFIED if: (a) Mann-Whitney p > 0.0125 (Bonferroni-corrected for 4 hypotheses, family alpha=0.05) comparing PLA2 gap of full_action vs baseline at k=4 pooled across n=64,128 (10 seeds each), OR (b) effect size (median difference / pooled MAD) < 0.5 (medium Cohen's d equivalent). Both conditions must hold to support.

claim: The Lagrangian geometrizability (lagr_geo_r2) increases with frob_from_rank1 across configs and scales, indicating that structured macro kernels admit local action descriptions in emergent coordinates
falsification_criterion: FALSIFIED if: Spearman rho between lagr_geo_r2 and frob_from_rank1, computed across all (config, seed, k) triples at n=128, is either (a) not significantly positive after Bonferroni correction (p > 0.0125), OR (b) rho < 0.3 (weak correlation). Both conditions must hold to support.

claim: full_action configs produce significantly lower spectral gap_ratio (higher t_rel) than baseline at n=128 k=4, indicating emergent approximate conservation laws. Original test used noether_modes (absolute eigenvalue threshold), which is uninformative. Reformulated to use gap_ratio and t_rel from spectral conservation probes. REVIEWER NOTE: this is a partial goalpost shift — old noether_modes targeted mode multiplicity, new gap_ratio/t_rel targets only the slowest timescale. Spectral participation and eigenvalue entropy should be checked as secondary evidence for mode multiplicity claims.
falsification_criterion: FALSIFIED if: (a) Mann-Whitney p > 0.0125 (Bonferroni-corrected) comparing gap_ratio of full_action vs baseline at k=4 with n=128, 10 seeds, OR (b) median gap_ratio difference < 0.1 (minimum meaningful effect). Both conditions must hold to support. Secondary: spectral_participation and eigen_entropy should be compared to assess mode-multiplicity aspect.

claim: Configs with partition competition (A14, A16, A17) produce significantly lower diffusion KL fit (lagr_diff_kl) than baseline, because partition competition produces macro kernels more closely resembling simple diffusion processes. NOTE: claim is 'lower than baseline', not 'lowest among all configs' — criterion tests A14 vs baseline with A16/A17 as replication.
falsification_criterion: FALSIFIED if: (a) Mann-Whitney p > 0.0125 (Bonferroni-corrected, 4 hypotheses) comparing lagr_diff_kl of A14_only vs baseline at k=4, n=128, 10 seeds, OR (b) median KL reduction < 20% relative to baseline (minimum meaningful effect). Both conditions must hold to support. Secondary: A16_only and A17_only tested separately as replication.

claim: Leave-one-out ablation from full_action identifies a generating set of <=6 cells (hypothesized: A13, A14, A18, A19 plus at most 2 others) that is both necessary and sufficient for the REV/high-frob regime. 'Necessary' = removing any one drops frob >30% or eliminates REV. 'Sufficient' = the subset alone achieves >80% of full_action median frob at n=128.
falsification_criterion: FALSIFIED if: (a) no single leave-one-out config shows >30% frob drop vs full_action (p<0.05, Mann-Whitney, 10 seeds), implying no cell is essential; OR (b) every tested subset of <=6 cells achieves <60% of full_action frob, implying the system requires >6 cells for emergence.

claim: Individual PICA cells produce distinct diagnostic signatures when enabled alone relative to baseline.
result: Wave_1 EXP-100 retest: 6/12 tested single-cell toggles (A3,A8,A9,A11,A12,A13) show at least one significant effect with |Cliff's delta|>0.5 and p<0.05 across {frob,sigma,macro_gap,sigma_ratio,budget} and n={32,64,128}. The other 6/12 are not distinguishable under this criterion. A10 is baseline and not isolatable in EXP-100.

claim: Multi-level PICA dynamics produce qualitatively richer structure than static macro derivation.
result: Wave_1 includes EXP-101 but does not include the static-control comparator required by the hypothesis text (EXP-090 baseline). Claim remains open pending direct controlled comparison.

claim: A19 boost parameter acts as a binary REV switch with boost-invariant outcomes.
result: Wave_1 EXP-106 retest does not reproduce binary invariance. At n=64, boost configs have REV rates {0.0,0.1} and non-zero sigma medians (1.48 to 5.54). At n=32, sigma differs significantly across boosts (Kruskal p=0.0447). The prior 100% REV/invariant claim is not supported on post-fix data.

claim: SBRC breaks the reversible chain regime (chain_SBRC becomes non-REV while chain stays REV).
result: Wave_1 EXP-106 retest: baseline chain (A13_A18_A19) is already mostly non-REV at n=64 (1/10 REV). chain_SBRC also 1/10 REV. Sigma increase is small and non-significant (Mann-Whitney one-sided p=0.312 at n=64). No clear chain->SBRC REV-breaking transition is observed.

claim: A14 disrupts the reversible A13_A18_A19 chain by increasing sigma and reducing REV.
result: Wave_1 EXP-106 retest does not support disruption. At n=64, A13_A14_A19 has lower median sigma than A13_A18_A19 (0.558 vs 1.307), and REV counts are similar (2/10 vs 1/10). One-sided sigma test for A14>chain is non-significant (p=0.575).

claim: At high-frob coarse scales, full_action has smaller PLA2 gap than baseline (saddle-point dominated paths).
result: Wave_1 EXP-109: k=4 pooled n=64,128 gives PLA2 median 0.218 (full_action, n=15) vs 0.564 (baseline, n=19), median diff=-0.346, Mann-Whitney one-sided p=1.58e-05, Cliff's delta=-0.846. Direction and magnitude support the claim.

claim: Lagrangian geometrizability (lagr_geo_r2) increases with frob across configs/scales.
result: Wave_1 EXP-109 at n=128: Spearman correlation between lagr_geo_r2 and frob across (config,seed,k) points is negative (rho=-0.233, p=1.99e-09, n=649). Observed direction is opposite to claim.

claim: full_action produces lower spectral gap_ratio (higher relaxation time) than baseline at n=128,k=4.
result: Wave_1 EXP-109: gap_ratio median 0.366 (full_action) vs 0.503 (baseline), median diff=-0.137, Mann-Whitney one-sided p=1.65e-04, Cliff's delta=-0.96. Effect exceeds the practical threshold (|diff|>0.1).

claim: Partition-competition single-lens configs (A14/A16/A17) have lower diffusion KL fit than baseline.
result: Wave_1 EXP-109 at n=128,k=4 shows the opposite: lagr_diff_kl medians are higher than baseline for A14 (0.182 vs 0.055, one-sided p=0.999), A16 (0.170 vs 0.055, p=0.998), and A17 (0.185 vs 0.055, p=0.999).

claim: Individual PICA cells produce distinct diagnostic signatures when enabled alone relative to baseline.
result: Wave_1 EXP-100 re-evaluated with multiplicity control: per-cell BH-FDR over 15 tests (5 metrics x 3 scales) plus |Cliff's delta|>0.5. Distinct cells are 2/12 (A12_P2-P6, A9_P2-P3). The earlier 6/12 count from uncorrected p<0.05 was overstated.

claim: A14 disrupts the reversible A13_A18_A19 chain by increasing sigma and reducing REV.
result: Support criterion enforced as conjunction (sigma increase AND REV drop). At n=64, sigma is lower for A13_A14_A19 than chain (median diff -0.749, one-sided p=0.575) and REV rates are similar (2/10 vs 1/10; Fisher p=0.895). Claim remains refuted.

claim: At high-frob coarse scales, full_action has smaller PLA2 gap than baseline (saddle-point dominated paths).
result: Strict preregistered criteria satisfied at k=4 pooled n=64,128: Mann-Whitney one-sided p=1.58e-05 (<0.0125 Bonferroni), median diff=-0.346 (full_action lower), pooled-MAD effect=3.05 (>=0.5).

claim: Lagrangian geometrizability (lagr_geo_r2) increases with frob_from_rank1.
result: Primary fixed-timescale test at n=128,k=4 is opposite-sign: Spearman rho=-0.237, p=0.00703. This fails preregistered direction/magnitude criteria (rho>=0.3 and positive). Secondary pooled and partial(k-controlled) analyses are also negative.

claim: full_action produces lower spectral gap_ratio (higher t_rel) than baseline at n=128,k=4.
result: Strict preregistered criteria satisfied: one-sided Mann-Whitney p=1.65e-04 (<0.0125), median gap_ratio diff=-0.137 (<=-0.1 threshold), Cliff's delta=-0.96.

claim: Partition-competition single-lens configs have lower lagr_diff_kl than baseline.
result: Primary A14_only vs baseline at n=128,k=4 is opposite-sign: median diff=+0.128 with one-sided p=0.9995 and relative reduction=-233.7% (required >=20% reduction). Replication configs A16_only and A17_only are also opposite-sign.
